description_long: This object is a wooden model of a classic biplane, featuring two parallel wings stacked one above the other and connected by vertical struts. The fuselage is slender and tapers towards the tail, which includes a rounded vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with a simple, flat design. At the front, a large, two-bladed propeller is mounted on a cylindrical nose, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The surface is unfinished, light-colored wood, giving it a natural, matte appearance. The construction is visibly modular, with interlocking flat pieces forming the wings, fuselage, and tail. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, such as those used in the era of barnstorming or World War I.
description: A light wooden biplane model with stacked wings, a front propeller, and simple landing gear. The surface is natural, matte wood with visible interlocking construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, large propeller, and symmetrical landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, open cockpit area, and the tail's rounded stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1 but from the other side, with clear visibility of the landing gear and propeller.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston propeller engine (model representation, no visible engine details).
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the propeller, single-seat configuration.
